figures.caption: Fig. 1. Overview of the proposed pipeline. Details in section 2. The top two rows belong to the same batch, whereas the bottom row is from another hemisphere processed under a different setting. WSI: Whole-Slide Images, LFB-OD: Luxol Fast Blue Optical Density, Norm.: Normalized. (I) white matter regions are not masked red, and delineated areas with lines are white matter hyperintensities; used for cross-modal evaluation(Section 2.2).

Fig. 2. Validation of CLAM-based reference region selection against manual expert placement across 84 slides. Left: raw Luxol Fast Blue Optical Density(LFB-OD) values (Pearson r = 0.91). Right: percentile-transformed values (Pearson r = 0.85).

Fig. 3. Distribution of Luxol Fast Blue Optical Density by expert pathology grade (0 = intact, 3 = severe myelin loss; n = 97 ROIs) across four quantification methods. More details in section 3.2. Statistical comparisons: ∗ p < 0.05, ∗ ∗ p < 0.01, ∗ ∗ ∗ p < 0.001, ∗ ∗ ∗ ∗ p < 0.0001.

Table 1. Cross-modal evaluation of Luxol Fast Blue Optical Density against T2w ex vivo MRI across 151 histology sections. Values are reported as ×10−2 (mean ± SD). Across all normalized methods, 148 of 151 sections (98%) achieved significant voxel-wise correlation (p < 0.05) within white matter, and 108 of 125 sections (86%) within WMH regions. Best shift ρ: best Spearman correlation achievable within a ±3- pixel spatial shift, quantifying sensitivity to residual registration error. Best values per metric in bold.
